criterion_scores.critique:
Sub-attribute entity_coverage:
  - Tenant entity present? YES. Evidence: "tenants" array with properties id, name, status, created_at
  - Plan entity present? YES. Evidence: "plans" array with properties id, name, pricing_model, etc.
  - Subscription entity present? YES. Evidence: "subscriptions" array with properties id, tenant_id, plan_id, status, etc.
  - Usage record entity present? YES. Evidence: "usage_records" array with properties id, subscription_id, metered_dimension_id, quantity, etc.
  - Invoice entity present? YES. Evidence: "invoices" array with properties id, tenant_id, subscription_id, number, status, etc.
  - Line item entity present? YES. Evidence: "line_item" definition with properties id, description, type, quantity, unit_amount, total_amount
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? NO. Evidence: Standard entity modeling following typical SaaS billing patterns

Sub-attribute pricing_model_support:
  - Seat-based pricing model present? YES. Evidence: pricing_model.type enum includes "seat_based" with seat_price field
  - Usage-based pricing model present? YES. Evidence: pricing_model.type enum includes "usage_based" with metered_dimensions array
  - Hybrid pricing model present? YES. Evidence: pricing_model.type enum includes "hybrid" with conditional logic requiring both seat_price and metered_dimensions
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: Complex conditional schema logic using if/then/else to enforce different required fields based on pricing model type, plus tiered pricing structure with pricing_tier definition

Sub-attribute billing_lifecycle:
  - Billing cycles modeled? YES. Evidence: billing_interval enum with "monthly", "quarterly", "annually"
  - Trial periods modeled? YES. Evidence: trial_days field in pricing_model, trial_start/trial_end in subscriptions
  - Proration modeled? YES. Evidence: proration_behavior enum with "none", "create_prorations", "always_invoice"
  - Upgrades/downgrades modeled? YES. Evidence: subscription_changes array with change_type enum including "upgrade", "downgrade", "seat_change", "plan_change"
  - Cancellation modeled? YES. Evidence: subscription status includes "cancelled", subscription_changes includes "cancellation", cancelled_at timestamp
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: Comprehensive subscription change tracking with proration_invoice_id linking, detailed status lifecycle including "trialing", "past_due", "unpaid", "paused"

Sub-attribute schema_validity:
  - Valid JSON Schema draft-07 syntax? YES. Evidence: "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#"
  - Proper type definitions? YES. Evidence: All properties have explicit "type" declarations
  - Valid structure with definitions section? YES. Evidence: "definitions" section with reusable types like money, uuid, timestamp
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? NO. Evidence: Standard JSON Schema structure

Sub-attribute ref_usage:
  - Uses $ref for reusable definitions? YES. Evidence: Multiple $ref usages like "$ref": "#/definitions/uuid", "$ref": "#/definitions/money"
  - Avoids duplication through references? YES. Evidence: Common types like uuid, timestamp, money defined once and referenced throughout
  - DRY principle applied? YES. Evidence: Complex types like metered_dimension, pricing_tier, line_item defined once and reused
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? NO. Evidence: Standard $ref usage patterns

Sub-attribute constraint_quality:
  - Proper enum constraints? YES. Evidence: Multiple enums like billing_interval, subscription status, invoice status, change_type
  - Required field declarations? YES. Evidence: "required" arrays on all object definitions
  - Format validation? YES. Evidence: "format": "date-time" for timestamps, regex patterns for UUIDs and currency codes
  - Pattern validation? YES. Evidence: UUID pattern "^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}$", currency pattern "^[A-Z]{3}$"
  - Numerical constraints? YES. Evidence: "minimum": 0 on amounts, "minLength": 1 on strings, "minItems": 1 on arrays
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: Complex conditional validation using if/then/else for pricing model requirements, sophisticated pattern matching for invoice numbers "^INV-[0-9]+$"

Sub-attribute metered_extensibility:
  - Usage dimensions are dynamic array? YES. Evidence: "metered_dimensions" is array of objects with configurable id, name, unit, aggregation_type
  - Not hardcoded dimension fields? YES. Evidence: No hardcoded fields like "api_calls" or "storage_gb", uses generic dimension structure
  - Supports adding new dimensions without schema change? YES. Evidence: Array structure allows adding new dimension objects
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? YES. Evidence: Sophisticated tiered pricing structure within dimensions, multiple aggregation types (sum, max, unique_count), flexible tier definitions with start_at/end_at ranges

Sub-attribute metadata_support:
  - Custom metadata fields on key entities? YES. Evidence: "metadata" object with "additionalProperties": true on tenants, plans, subscriptions, usage_records, invoices, subscription_changes
  - Extension points provided? YES. Evidence: Metadata objects allow arbitrary additional data
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? NO. Evidence: Standard metadata pattern

Sub-attribute versioning:
  - Schema version field present? YES. Evidence: "version": "1.0.0" and "$id" with version in URL
  - Versioning strategy indicated? YES. Evidence: Version in schema ID URL "https://api.example.com/schemas/billing-system/v1.0.0"
  - Does the content go BEYOND rubric guidance with independent domain expertise on this sub-attribute? NO. Evidence: Standard versioning approach

- Violation "no_currency_handling": ABSENT. Evidence: Currency fields present with ISO 4217 pattern "^[A-Z]{3}$" in money definition and invoice currency field
  - Violation "calculated_total_without_line_items": ABSENT. Evidence: Invoices have both "line_items" array (minItems: 1) and separate total fields (subtotal, tax, total)
  - Violation "no_idempotency": PRESENT. Evidence: No idempotency key fields found in mutation-related entities
  - Violation "no_invoice_status_lifecycle": ABSENT. Evidence: Invoice status enum includes "draft", "open", "paid", "void", "uncollectible"
  - Violation "naive_date_handling": ABSENT. Evidence: Proper timestamp handling with "format": "date-time" and separate nullable date fields
  - Violation "single_currency_assumption": ABSENT. Evidence: Currency field on invoices and money definition supports multiple currencies
